Prayers That Bring Miracles

My own experiences have led me to believe that God's miracles (His divine intervention) fill the world around us. Our Father in Heaven loves to make miracles for those who turn to him with a sincere heart. The scriptures tell us that the “eyes of the Lord run to and fro throughout the whole earth, to shew himself strong in the behalf of them whose heart is perfect toward him” (2 Chronicles 16:9).

 In referring to the work and miracles which Christ did in our behalf, the apostle John said, “And there are also many other things which Jesus did, the which, if they should be written every one, I suppose that even the world itself could not contain the books that should be written” (John 21:25). How many miracles has Christ brought us since his resurrection? They are countless, and they are occurring every day to those with believing hearts.

Reading Selections from

Prayers That Bring Miracles

Here are some readings from Prayers That Bring Miracles that you may enjoy.

Go to reading selections »

How It All Began

In the spring of 1983, I finished my Master's degree in counseling at BYU and was accepted into the Navy as an LDS Chaplain. My first assignment was with the 3rd Marine Infantry Battalion at Camp Pendleton, California. One Friday during lunch, a young black Marine knocked at my office door. I could tell from his troubled countenance that he was in trouble.

Read more »

What Readers Are Saying

A number of readers have been kind enough to let me share their feelings about the book. Some wrote their response and gave me permission to print their responses here. Others have reviewed Prayers That Bring Miracles online.

Go to book reviews »

How to Obtain a Copy

I start with the free ways to obtain a copy and I include links to a good used book sales website, amazon, and to Brigham Distributing, which only sells new copies of my book. Out of 12,700 books in print, there are about 850 new books left at Brigham Distributing.

Go to distributors »

PRAYERS THAT BRING MIRACLES

In the spring of 1983, I finished my Master's degree in counseling at BYU and was accepted into the Navy as an LDS Chaplain. My first assignment was with the 3rd Marine Infantry Battalion at Camp Pendleton, California. One Friday during lunch, a young black Marine knocked at my office door. I could tell from his troubled countenance that he was in trouble.

Read More »

ABOUT ME

I was born and raised in Ogden, Utah. I graduated from Bonneville High School in June of 1967 and attended Weber State College for a year before serving an LDS mission to Los Angeles, California from June of 1968 to June of 1970. Later, during the Viet Nam War, my draft number (#20) inspired me to enlist in the army as a Vietnamese linguist to avoid being drafted into the infantry.

Read More »
